From what I can see there isn't any possibility of further shortening the article without removing key information. Most articles on wikipedia are far beyond what the guidelines currently say. This article is 115,000 words not including reference list, the guidelines say 15,000 words max; it's just not possible without destroying the article
In response to need to shorten article, removed parts that were either uncited or seemed unnecessary or unencyclopedic. Also added some CNs. REVERT if you disagree with any of these removals.
Proper SD date format for other conflicts

According to the template, the apostrophe with a small leading space is supposed to be used just following bold or italic text, not before it. I removed the normal-sized space so there isn't a huge gap, but ultimately that space should be restored and the same kind of apostrophe without a leading space should be inserted, but I don't know how to do that.
there is no reason for Westmoreland's name to be nowrapped; nowrap should be used sparingly and this is not a good use case

I don't know who keeps editing this, but they're either functionally illiterate or have a political agenda. 667,000 is not a lower bound for total communist military deaths. It is specifically a secondhand estimate covering 10 years of a 20 year conflict. The only number spanning its totality is the ~1.08 million killed/missing put out by the PAVN itself.
